title: Hyderabad set to get some respite from heatwave, at least for a few days - The Siasat Daily
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
full_text:
Hyderabad: Amid rising temperatures, Telangana is set to get respite from heat after April 18 as thunderstorms are forecast in several districts.
Hyderabad is expected to receive thunder showers from April 19 to 23.
According to T Balaji, a weather enthusiast, districts such as Vikarabad, Rangareddy, Sangareddy, Medak are expected to receive thunderstorms, mostly in the evening.
Other parts of Telangana are likely to experience the same from April 20 to 24.
Telangana government issues advisory
As the India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecasts above normal heatwave in Telangana, the state government on Wednesday, April 15, issued an advisory for the public to take precautions.
The circular highlighted that IMD has already issued Orange Alerts for multiple districts, with extreme heat conditions expected to persist and intensify through May and early June 2026.
The government warned that heatwaves pose a “silent but deadly risk,” disproportionately affecting the elderly, children, pregnant women, outdoor workers in agriculture, construction and sanitation, urban poor and slum dwellers and livestock.
Cooling centres, chalivendrams
District Disaster Management Authorities (DDMA) have been instructed to activate cooling centres at public buildings, including schools, Anganwadi centres, community halls and panchayat offices. They have also been asked to support the opening of “chalivendrams,” or traditional drinking water and buttermilk stations, at places of worship, malls, bus stands and other public congregation points.
The circular mandates that DDMAs hold regular press conferences on heat-related illness risks and ensure oral rehydration solution (ORS) packets are available at chalivendrams and water camps across heatwave-affected districts.
School Advisories and Work timing
Since there is an orange alert in Telangana, outdoor workers are to be advised to avoid work during the peak afternoon hours of noon to 3 pm. Anganwadi centres are to run only until 11 am.
Schools have been directed to avoid open-air classes, consider reduced hours or closures and schedule “water bells” for mandatory hydration breaks.
Multi-department mobilisation
The government has called for a coordinated effort between various department The Energy Department has been asked to prioritise uninterrupted electricity supply to hospitals, water supply units and cooling centres, and to set up emergency generators at vulnerable locations.
Hospitals have been directed to establish toll-free heatwave helplines, stock reusable ice packs, organise workshops for medical students and provide separate beds for sunstroke victims.
The Labour Department is required to conduct awareness camps for factory managers and outdoor workers and to regulate construction sites to ensure drinking water, ORS and shelter are provided to labourers.
The Animal Husbandry Department has been asked to set up shaded areas and cool water tubs for animals in public and rural areas.

title: Heatwave tightens grip in Telangana as nine districts record above 43℃; over 42℃ in 22 districts - The Hindu

full_text:
Heatwave-like conditions continue to intensify across Telangana, with temperatures rising steadily in most parts of the State. On Wednesday (April 15, 2026), as many as nine districts recorded maximum temperatures of 43C or above.
According to data from the Telangana Development Planning Society (TGDPS), the bulk of the State remained in the high-temperature band, with 22 districts registering temperatures between 42℃ and 43℃. Only two districts recorded temperatures below 42℃, including Hyderabad, where the maximum stood at 41.4℃.
Published - April 16, 2026 01:11 pm IST

full_text:
Home |Hyderabad |Telangana Rolls Out Heatwave Action Plan Schools To Implement Water Bells Cooling Centres Set Up
Telangana rolls out heatwave action plan; schools to implement water bells, cooling centres set up
Telangana has announced a comprehensive heatwave action plan, mandating water bells in schools and reduced Anganwadi hours, while designating malls and public buildings as cooling centres. Authorities have also issued an Orange Alert with temperatures expected up to 44°C.
Hyderabad: As Telangana prepare for heatwave, all schools have been instructed to implement ‘water bells’ and malls, religious places and public buildings have been designated as ‘cooling centres’ for the general public seeking reprieve from the hot sun.
It is now mandatory for schools to provide ‘water bells’, with breaks to ensure the students are well hydrated. Schools have also been asked not to hold open-air classes, which will ensure children do not come in direct contact with sun. The Aanganwadi centers across Hyderabad and State will now operate only until 11 am
Malls and shops have been instructed by the State government to keep cold water at their premises, while religious places, government buildings, and panchayat offices have been designated as cooling centres, where the public can go and rest during the heat wave. It has also been decided to install green shades at traffic junctions and public gatherings.
Health Minister, Damodar Raja Narasimha issued a comprehensive set of instructions to District Collectors to mitigate the risks of heatwave. Beyond students and the general public, special focus is on ‘gig economy workers, with specific outreach planned for auto-drivers, cab drivers, and delivery workers who are at high risk during peak afternoon hours.
All the government hospitals should have a dedicated heatstroke team in the emergency area to begin treatment immediately upon a patient’s arrival. The Minister has also directed the electricity department to frame a summer-specific power load plan to ensure uninterrupted electricity to hospitals and water supply units, while the Fire Department conducts audits of chemical godowns to prevent heat-induced fires.
The Telangana State Development Planning Society (TGDPS) will now provide Mandal-level, two-day forecasts focusing on the Heat Index, which is a combination of temperature and humidity, and to be shared directly to local nodal officers.
Authorities have warned of an ‘Orange Alert’ condition already prevailing in several districts, with May and early June expected to see sustained maximum temperatures between 41 degree Celsius and 44 degree Celsius.
Residents are urged to avoid outdoor activities between 12 noon and 3 pm and utilize the newly established ‘Chalivendrams’ (drinking water kiosks) being set up across urban and rural congregation points, authorities urged.
Malls, public buildings and religious places to serve water and cooling centers
Mandatory water bells in schools and avoid open-air classes
Aanganwadi centres to be shut down by 11 am
Shift schedule of outdoor workers from 12 noon to 3 pm during heart wave alert
Fire department to conduct security check at commercial complexes and hospitals
Shaded areas and cool water tubs for animals in urban and rural areas

title: Telangana Heatwave Relief: Thunderstorms Expected From April 18-24, Hyderabad Included - Telangana Tribune

full_text:
Telangana is set to receive much-needed relief from the ongoing intense heatwave as thunderstorms are expected to sweep across the state starting April 18-19. The weather activity will initially affect districts including Vikarabad, Rangareddy, Sangareddy, and Medak before extending to most parts of the state between April 20-24. The forecast brings hope to residents who have been enduring severe heat conditions over the past several days.
The anticipated thunderstorms are expected to occur primarily during evening and night hours. This means that daytime temperatures will continue to remain very hot until the evening sets in. Residents are advised to take necessary precautions during the day as the scorching heat will persist until the thunderstorm activity begins in the later part of the day.
The state capital Hyderabad is also expected to experience thunderstorms and subsequent relief from the heatwave during April 19-23. The weather system will provide temporary respite to the city’s residents who have been grappling with extreme temperatures in recent weeks. The evening and night thunderstorms are likely to bring down the mercury levels across the city.
The extended period of thunderstorm activity from April 20-24 is expected to cover most districts of Telangana. This widespread weather phenomenon will likely provide relief across the state, though the intensity and duration may vary from region to region. The weather pattern indicates a typical pre-monsoon thunderstorm activity that often provides temporary relief from summer heat.
Weather authorities have issued alerts regarding the expected thunderstorm activity across various districts of Telangana. The forecast suggests that while the thunderstorms will bring relief from the heat, residents should remain cautious during the weather events. The combination of hot daytime temperatures followed by evening thunderstorms is characteristic of the transition period before the monsoon season arrives in the region.

title: State Orders Heatstroke Preparedness in Hospitals Amid Rising Temperatures - Deccan Chronicle
full_text:
State Orders Heatstroke Preparedness in Hospitals Amid Rising Temperatures
Citing the risk of power disruptions during summer storms, he asked officials to ensure that generators are functional in all hospitals and undergo regular maintenance.
Hyderabad:In response to rising summer temperatures, health minister Damodar Rajanarasimha on Wednesday directed officials to ensure immediate treatment for heatstroke patients and strengthen preparedness across government hospitals.
At a review meeting at the Rajiv Aarogyasri Trust office in Jubilee Hills, the minister instructed that every hospital deploy a dedicated team in emergency wards to handle heatstroke cases. He called for special arrangements, including provision of drinking water, installation of fans, coolers and air-conditioners where required, and proper ventilation in wards and waiting areas.
Officials were directed to set up drinking water facilities on every floor and in every block of major hospitals, and to arrange shaded rest areas within hospital premises.
Senior officials informed the minister that essential medicines, including ORS, are available and that arrangements are in place to provide immediate treatment to heatstroke patients. The minister said trained emergency teams should be kept ready at all times to ensure prompt care on patient arrival.
Citing the risk of power disruptions during summer storms, he asked officials to ensure that generators are functional in all hospitals and undergo regular maintenance.
Raising concerns over fire hazards, the minister directed authorities to take preventive measures, including inspection of power cables to avoid short circuits and replacement of old wiring where necessary. Hospitals were also asked to conduct fire safety mock drills, ensure availability of fire extinguishers, check their expiry dates, and train staff and security personnel in their use.
